final_answer:
Most chains do validator-set changes at fixed boundaries (block/epoch/round), not with a continuously live denominator during an active approval round.

- Ethereum PoS: validator entry is permissionless via deposit, then activation queue + churn limit; it is not a live K-of-N admission vote.  
  Source: https://ethereum.github.io/consensus-specs/specs/phase0/beacon-chain/
- Polkadot: NPoS selects validators; candidates can sit in a waiting queue, and the validator set refreshes each era.  
  Sources: https://docs.polkadot.com/reference/polkadot-hub/consensus-and-security/pos-consensus/ , https://docs.polkadot.com/node-infrastructure/run-a-validator/onboarding-and-offboarding/start-validating/
- Cosmos/CometBFT: validator updates are produced at end-block and applied with deterministic next-block semantics (H+1/H+2 flow), not continuously mid-block.  
  Sources: https://docs.cosmos.network/sdk/v0.47/build/building-modules/beginblock-endblock , https://docs.cometbft.com/v0.37/spec/abci/abci++_methods
- Permissioned chains: governance/admin-driven membership is common (for example Cosmos PoA admin authority; Besu IBFT/QBFT validator governance patterns).  
  Sources: https://docs.cosmos.network/enterprise/components/poa/overview , https://lf-hyperledger.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BESU/overview

Why it matters for Gold Chain: this is exactly why your `>2/3` validator admission should use a fixed per-round snapshot, so validator churn cannot manipulate thresholds or deadlock approvals.

What should be done next:
1. Keep denominator and voter eligibility fixed for one admission round.  
2. Recompute only when starting the next round.  
3. Add expiry/rollover so any stalled round closes and restarts cleanly with a fresh snapshot.
prompt: how do other chains do it? research web and then respond
